Provides limited basic personal care items such as soap, shampoo, and toothpaste 13565 SW Walker Road Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Call Center Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm Walk in hours: Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 9am-1pm, Monday/Thursday by appointment only Email (503) 591-9025 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY HEALTH PARTNERS URGENT CARE CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers lab testing, medications and point of service testing such as basic urinalysis, blood glucose, strep throat testing, blood pressure screening, and pregnancy testing. Immunizations for seasonal flu and Tdap (tetanus, whooping cough) are also available. 945 Washington Way Suite 141 Longview, WA 98632 Eligibility: Uninsured adults age 18 and older Hours: Tuesday 9am-noon, Wednesday 5pm-8pm, Thursday noon-3pm Email (360) 442-4165 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FORT KENNEDY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes, pet food, clothing, showers, blood pressure checks, bus passes, legal information services, and benefit and housing resources. 7600 SE Johnson Creek Boulevard Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Veterans, all discharge types Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm. Various programs may have specific times. Email (503) 765-2661 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HARNEY COUNTY HARNEY HUB SENIOR AND COMMUNITY CENTER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides utility assistance for gas, electric, and heating fuel bills; motel vouchers; one-time gas vouchers; transportation passes; food pantry (visit twice per month); Fresh Alliance program (provides fresh produce, dairy, and bread products); weatherization services; and blood pressure screenings. Offers snack kits. Assistive medical equipment and mobility aids also available. 17 S Alder Street Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: -Food pantry: Households at or below 300% federal poverty level. No proof of income is required. -Fresh Alliance: Unrestricted -Snack kits for those looking to keep moving through the community -Motel vouchers: Homeless individuals requiring one night's stay -Gas vouchers: Must be stuck and looking to continue to move through the community -Transportation passes: Must have no ties to the community and looking to continue to move through the community -Energy Assistance: Household must meet federal poverty guidelines Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm Fresh Alliance: Monday 10am-10:30am Food pantry: Wednesday 10am-1pm, Thursday 4pm-6pm Email (541) 573-6024 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE FILIPINO BAYANIHAN COMMUNITY BASED HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides blood pressure and blood sugar screenings. Offers herbal medicine making, massage, bodywork, and health education. 1537 SE Morrison Street Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Filipino Community Hours: 2nd Friday of the month 6pm-8pm Email (971) 895-3917 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WILLAMALANE PARKS AND RECREATION DISTRICT ADULT HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weekly blood pressure checks, foot care, and education from retired nurses. Free tax preparation is available for older community members with low income.
